title: Born-rule statistical dynamical quantum phase transitions under measurement
abstract: Dynamical quantum phase transitions (DQPTs) occur at times when a quantum state exhibits a nonanalytic change in its return probability. This can be viewed as the probability of collapsing the evolved state to the initial state by quantum measurement. However, the initial wave function usually has exponentially small amplitude in the late time evolved state. Here we perform statistical characterization for all the possible post-measurement states distributed according to the Born's rule, by sampling a one-dimensional quantum Ising chain after a quantum quench dynamics. The statistical ensemble can also be viewed as a mixed state when the time evolved state is subjected to maximally dephasing noise in a certain basis. We map the distribution to a statistical model and characterize its effective "energy" spectrum, and introduce the average dynamical free energy, establishing a framework for the statistical DQPTs. We show the recovering of DQPT under high-moment average and a delocalized level distribution following critical times. Through analytic continuation into the complex time plane, we demonstrate the vanishing of Yang-Lee-Fisher zeros and the emergent level crossing near critical times. Finally, we propose a measurement-based quantum computation protocol to simulate the unitary evolution via single-qubit measurements on a two-dimensional cluster state. Our results provide a way for experimentally investigating statistical DQPTs in quantum devices, shedding light on the structured circuit sampling with insights from DQPT and generalizing the understanding of mixed state due to decoherence beyond equilibrium.
summary: This work develops a statistical framework to study Dynamical Quantum Phase Transitions (DQPTs) by analyzing the ensemble of states resulting from quantum measurements on an Ising chain. By using moment averaging of the dynamical free energy, the authors show that while single trajectories exhibit sharp nonanalyticities, the statistical average smooths these out. This provides a crucial theoretical tool for interpreting experimental measurements in quantum devices.
main_problem: The paper investigates Dynamical Quantum Phase Transitions (DQPTs) by analyzing the statistical ensemble of post-measurement quantum states, moving beyond single-trajectory analysis.
main_result: The statistical average (Born rule) of the ensemble washes out the nonanalytic signatures of DQPTs seen in single trajectories, but the framework successfully characterizes statistical DQPTs using the average dynamical free energy.
method: The authors characterize the ensemble using the $n$-th moment average dynamical free energy ($f_n$), mapping the distribution to a statistical model, and employing analytic continuation into the complex time plane.
model_or_system: The primary model is the one-dimensional transverse-field Ising chain, subjected to a quantum quench followed by measurement, which induces a mixed state described by the Born probability distribution.
key_observables: Average dynamical free energy ($f_n$), multifractal dimensions ($D_q$), and the vanishing/emergence of Yang-Lee-Fisher zeros in the complex time plane.
important_parameters: The moment index $n$ acts as an effective inverse temperature, allowing crossover from high-temperature ($n=0$) to zero-temperature ($n	o\infty$) limits.
main_assumptions: The analysis assumes the measurement-induced mixed state is distinct from standard finite-temperature mixed states, and that the system can be analyzed via moment averaging.
figures_summary: Figures illustrate the evolution of the dynamical free energy spectrum, showing level condensation near critical times, and compare the complex time evolution of post-selected vs. statistically averaged quantities, demonstrating the disappearance of zeros in the latter.
paper_structure: The paper first introduces the statistical characterization via $f_n$ for the Ising model. It then analyzes the crossover from multifractality to delocalization at the critical time. Finally, it uses analytic continuation to show how the Born-rule average affects the detection of DQPT signatures compared to single-trajectory methods.
why_interesting: It provides a rigorous statistical framework to study non-equilibrium quantum dynamics, showing how measurement fundamentally alters the observable signatures of quantum phase transitions, which is crucial for interpreting experimental quantum measurements.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks

figures.caption: FIG. 1. Dynamical free energy spectrum. (a) Schematic of the model: a quantum quench dynamics by Ising model in ferromagnetic phase followed by measurements in X basis. Repeating the protocol yields a Born-distributed ensemble of bitstrings σ, whose Born probability P(σ) ∝e−Lf(σ) defines the effective free energy f(σ), conditioned upon σ (the temporal boundary condition of spacetime path integral). (b) The evolution of post-selected dynamical free energy f(+⋯+) under PBC. The nonanalytic period t∗without transverse field is exactly π/2 labeled by vertical dashed lines. Finite-size (L = 12) evolution of e−f(+⋯+) and e−fn in the complex time plane τ ∈[−π/4, π/4]. (a), (d) Post-selected e−f(+⋯+) = P 1/L(+⋯+) : Periodic zeros discretely lie on the complex plane (not the real axis τ = 0 as the critical real time in Fig. 1(b)). (b), (e) Unit-temperature e−f1: Discrete zeros at τ ≠0 and nonanalytic kinks on the real axis are both washed out by averaging. (c), (f) Ground-state e−f∞: Dis- crete zeros in the complex plane are covered by lower-energy config- urations. Although the finite-size level crossing still remains in the plane, sufficiently...

FIG. 4. Bulk measurement protocol. Schematic representation of a 2D cluster state on the Lieb lattice, whose spatial periodic bound- ary is defined on a cylinder (left). The corresponding time evolu- tion operator of one Trotter step is U(∆t) = eihX∆teiJZZ∆t whose unit cell of projective measurement on a plaquette is shown on the right. Measurement bases are represented on the Bloch spheres as cos (θ/2) ∣0⟩+eiϕ sin(θ/2) ∣1⟩and positive (negative) measurement outcomes are denoted by red (blue) arrows. Corresponding angles related to the Ising model ignoring the time step are labeled beside arrows (mentioned as (θ, ϕ) in the text).

FIG. 5. Finite-size level inversion. Average dynamical free energy (a) f1 and (c) f5 in different system sizes around the first critical time with h = 0.2 (indicated by dashed lines). The analytic f(+⋯+) (black) is obtained by Eq. 6. Compared with f1, high-moment f5 showing a narrower half-height width lies closer to f(+⋯+). (b), (d): The difference between fn and analytic f(+⋯+) as a func- tion of 1/L is labeled by corresponding colors with the fitted linear equation. This can be regard as the gap between the low tempera- ture thermal state and paramagnetic state.
